author: omerfirmak
content: I don't see any of the fixtures having statelessInputBytes/witnesses. Is the idea to run these natively like a blockchain test?

author: jsign
content:
Sorry, yeah, there was a mistake. I forgot to cherry-pick the external t8n support from `projects/zkevm` into the test-zkevm@v0.4.1 since that feature wasn't part of that release yet, plus do some extra t8n changes in geth that hopefully I can propose upstreaming soon so I don't have to remember doing. Too many branches to juggle.

I [changed the link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1HDNyZ_kq-1_69mWBQT5oosMjLVfrnqBW/view?usp=sharing) in the original message with the new .tar.zstd -- now you should see `stateless{Input|Output}Bytes`.

author: omerfirmak
content: Nice, I will integrate these into CI.

author: jsign
content: Without rush and whenever you give it a try, LMK if all those benchmark "pass" just to have a sense on how "coherent" geth `glamsterdam-devnet-5` is as a source of truth for benchmark filling from your guest program perspective (i.e. cross checking two "EL"s at least for benchmark fixtures)

author: jsign
content: I already have some internal cross-check between specs and geth filling benchmarks for 1M gas limit (to be easy on python) targets that can prob catch 99.9% of potential mismatches -- but still curious about a third external cross check in this 60M gas limit.
